overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted professional social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing heavily on computational social science, disinformation, and academic opportunities. With a combined following of 3,580, his posts consistently generate strong engagement, particularly when announcing major research milestones, funding awards, and hiring opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Research updates, publications, and methodological discussions on detecting coordinated link sharing and information operations.
Job openings and academic opportunities, including postdoctoral hiring and PhD student recruitment.
Announcements regarding software updates (e.g., CooRnet v2.0) and newly awarded research grants.

recommendations: Cross-promote tool releases like CooRnet v2.0 across LinkedIn and Bluesky to leverage their larger and highly active audiences.
Utilize Bluesky's high repost-to-like ratio to amplify recruitment posts and call-for-papers to a broader, highly networked academic community.
Increase LinkedIn posting frequency to share more incremental research insights, as it has the largest follower base and generates the highest absolute volume of interactions.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky has the highest average engagement rate (6.14%) and shows exceptional virality for publication announcements and academic opportunities, driven by a highly active academic network that reposts frequently.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides steady engagement (4.55% average ER) from a dedicated niche community, showing strong interest in open-source research software updates like CooRnet.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn represents the largest audience base (1,850 followers) and yields the highest absolute engagement, proving highly effective for professional milestones, grant funding announcements, and recruitment.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
